keypoints.title: Scoring is based on three key performance areas

sections.title: Quality outcomes
sections.text: Measures hospital success at keeping patients healthy, ensuring survival, and avoiding readmission.

sections.title: Patient experience
sections.text: Based on patient feedback about things like cleanliness, comfort and care team efficiency.

sections.title: Patient safety
sections.text: Tells us how well hospitals avoid preventable errors and complications such as infections and bedsores.

about.title: About HealthLocator
about.content:
When searching for the best care, hospital ranking lists are helpful, but with dozens of lists available, research can be confusing. That’s where HealthLocator comes in.

HealthLocator is a searchable database built by Mayo Clinic to provide quality ratings for more than 5,000 hospitals across the nation. This easy-to-use tool gathers findings from various publicly available sources of hospital quality data. (See Where does the data come from?)

The information is vetted by external stakeholders, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) — a federal agency that works with the healthcare community to improve quality, equity, and outcomes across the nation — and HealthLocator’s own team of world-renowned data scientists. The resulting tool provides a broad view of hospital performance across several quality measurements.

Learn about our methods for measuring hospital quality, reliable excellence, and HealthLocator scoring.

whycreated.title: Why was HealthLocator created?
whycreated.content:
When searching for the best care, hospital ranking lists are helpful, but with dozens of lists available, research can be confusing. That’s where HealthLocator comes in.

HealthLocator is a searchable database of quality and ratings for more than 5,000 hospitals across the nation. This easy-to-use tool gathers findings from various publicly available sources of hospital quality data. (See Where does the data come from?)

The information is vetted by external stakeholders, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), and HealthLocator’s own team of world renowned data-research scientists. The resulting tool provides a broad view of hospital performance across several quality measurements.

HealthLocator aligns with HealthLocator’s mission to always prioritize patient needs by connecting people with other quality institutions that deliver great care.

what-is-being-scored.title: What is being scored?
what-is-being-scored.content:
The overall hospital score and hospital average scores shown in HealthLocator are based on the performance of each hospital across:

Quality outcomes: Measures hospital success at keeping patients healthy, ensuring survival, and avoiding readmission. This score is based on 5 different areas of quality outcomes.

Patient experience: Based on patient feedback about things like communication, comfort, and staff teamwork. This score is based on 6 different areas of patient experience.

Patient safety: Tells us how well hospitals avoid preventable errors and complications such as infections and bedsores. This score is based on 6 different areas of patient safety.

where-data-comes-from.title: Where does the data come from?
where-data-comes-from.content:
HealthLocator references the following resources:

Quality outcomes scoring is based on summary scores from the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Overall Hospital Star Rating to distinguish excellence in quality. CMS is a federal agency that works with the healthcare community to improve quality, equity, and outcomes across the nation.

Patient experience scoring is based on summary scores from the Hospital Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (HCAHPS) survey to distinguish excellence in patient experience. The HCAHPS survey is a national, standardized, publicly reported survey of patients’ perspectives of hospital care.

Patient safety scoring is based on six publicly reported hospital patient safety and associated infection metrics. This data allows us to emphasize the importance of patient safety.

how-are-hospitals-scored.title: How are hospitals scored?
how-are-hospitals-scored.content:
Hospital scoring happens in three steps.

Step 1: Hospital ratings in key areas

Each hospital is rated from 0 (lowest) to 99 (highest) in three key areas:

  • Quality care: How well it keeps patients healthy
  • Patient experience: How patients feel about their care
  • Patient safety: How well it protects patients from harm

For example, a hospital may receive ratings of 75 for quality care, 60 for patient experience, and 90 for patient safety.

Step 2: Hospital average score

These ratings are used to calculate the hospital average score. Using the numbers above, the hospital average score would be 75.

Step 3: Hospital overall score

The average score of 75 is then converted to a 0–99 scale (0=worst; 99=best) based on the highest scoring hospital. This gives us a hospital overall score of 93. That means the hospital is doing better than 93 out of 100 hospitals.

Learn about our methods for measuring hospital quality, reliable excellence, and HealthLocator scoring.
